divergence: Women gained the vote in 1848 and immediately voted for functioning plumbing
story_angle: solemn obituary for a beloved institution nobody can remember the purpose of
headline: Imperial Lodestone Alignment Bureau's Dissolution: A Stampede of Irrelevance
deck: As the six-jointed clerks depart, half-timbered structures remain inexplicably stamped.
article:
Beneath the Grand Assembly Hall's sagging beams, Druia residents marked the Imperial Lodestone Alignment Bureau's quiet end. The agency once validated plumbing ballots through magnetic alignment; its closure leaves every half-timbered building still requiring an annual stamp that no longer exists.

The 1848 voting incident exposed the clerks' six-jointed hands as the source of chronic misfilings. Magnetic purpose faded from memory long ago, yet the 1842 statutes still assign the absent Chief Hydraulic Engineer the duty of inspections.

Citizens now weigh whether tradition or plumbing will buckle first under the missing marks.
author: Beatrix Foggweather, Senior Correspondent of Bureaucratic Distinction

weather.condition: Rain expected; toilets still in demand.

oped.title: The Bureau's Noble Stamps Must Endure, Lest We All Drown in Unaligned Pipes
oped.author: Sir Thaddeus Quibbleton, Perpetual High Warden of Magnetic Waistcoats and Proprietor of the Only Correctly Stamped Gazebo
oped.body: Dissolving the Imperial Lodestone Alignment Bureau invites national disorder. The six-jointed clerks, for all their habit of double-stamping my neighbour while slighting my gazebo, kept the 1848 plumbing statutes intact. Without their marks, how will cottages resist the deluge of female suffrage and working drains? Replace them with eight-jointed machines that stamp thrice as often, preferably on the hats of anyone who ever doubted my alignment. The structures stay stamped because magnetic order persists; the clerks were simply a clerical mistake.

classifieds.text: Six-jointed lodestone calipers and stamping dies from the late Imperial Alignment Bureau, lightly used before the clerks' untimely exodus; apply to Widow Cratchit at No. 12 Half-Timber Lane, cash only, structures not included.

Urgent: three steady lads required to scrub inexplicable stamps from half-timbered gables before the next alignment eclipse; wages paid in functioning plumbing tokens, no experience with six-jointed persons necessary.
Lost: one brass lodestone alignment registry, last seen clutched by a dying clerk during the Stampede of Irrelevance; finder may keep the stamps if returned to the Gazette office forthwith.

The undersigned hereby apologizes for the rash of half-timbered structures stamped 'Irrelevant' during the Bureau's dissolution; blame lies with the clerks' mortal coil, not with this humble lodestone fancier, Mrs. Amelia Thorne.

panels.caption: 'Did you see the papers? Imperial Lodestone Alignment Bureau's Dissolution.'
'I saw them. I also saw our tea get cold while you read them aloud.'
'Progress, dear. The Empire waits for no one — except apparently us.'

joke.setup: The Imperial Lodestone Alignment Bureau dissolved yesterday in a stampede of irrelevance,
joke.punchline: its fellows having spent a decade aligning needles while the drains continued to align themselves with the Thames.
joke.text: The Imperial Lodestone Alignment Bureau dissolved yesterday in a stampede of irrelevance, its fellows having spent a decade aligning needles while the drains continued to align themselves with the Thames.

sponsor_ads.headline: Bureau Remnants Offered for Pipe Alignment
sponsor_ads.body: Following the Imperial Lodestone Alignment Bureau's dissolution in a general stampede of irrelevance, we offer surplus magnetic rods for ensuring the new water conduits mandated by the 1848 suffrage remain true despite magnetic interference from the Thames.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Not liable for floods induced by residual polarity.

sponsor_ads.headline: Public Notice: Stampede Training for Former Aligners
sponsor_ads.body: Gentlemen lately of the dissolved Lodestone Bureau may now learn the approved method of stampeding toward domestic plumbing inspection, the sole occupation rendered non-irrelevant by the ladies' 1848 vote.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Classes convene behind the new water closets at noon.

sponsor_ads.headline: Tonic Against the Stampede of Irrelevance
sponsor_ads.body: Procure Dr. Pettigrew's celebrated cordial, guaranteed to preserve a gentleman's sense of purpose now that lodestone work has been rendered superfluous by functioning pipes and the wiser sex's ballot.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Contains no actual magnetism; results not guaranteed after 1848.

sponsor_ads.headline: Wanted: Magnetic Housemaids for Post-Bureau Homes
sponsor_ads.body: Since the Imperial Lodestone Alignment Bureau dissolved amid irrelevance, ladies of the 1848 electorate seek servants versed in redirecting stray fields away from the newly plumbed water lines of modern residences.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Must provide own stays; no gentlemen need apply.

stories.headline: Alignment Contractors Petition for Emergency Stamps After Bureau Closure
stories.deck: Half-timbered warehouses now drift three degrees east weekly
stories.article:
Northern merchants filed for replacement lodestones after the bureau closed, noting shop fronts slipping from true bearing. Last month's contracts now carry four-silver-vein penalties for each misplaced beam.

One importer watched its warehouse of copper valves rotate overnight, breaching the 1848 orientation rules.

stories.headline: Six-Jointed Clerks' Exit Leaves Jubilee Ball Without Proper Seating Charts
stories.deck: Porcelain guests arrive facing magnetic west
stories.article:
The Magnetic North reception now relies on hand-drawn compasses instead of stamped charts. Vein-traced guests have asked to be moved from southern exposures.

Old invitations still list elongated fingers that once confirmed every direction.

real_world_echo: Scores feared dead after passenger ferry capsizes off Guyana mirrored as the Bureau's quiet institutional 'capsize' into irrelevance after magnetic errors doomed transport alignment
